page.content: Each timeframe is accompanied by P-values, which serve as indicators of statistical significance in relation to the traits or groups examined. Notably, the table highlights that for the EZH2 grade 0 category, a consistent 100% prevalence was observed across both four-year periods, accompanied by P-values of 0.6324 and 0.1125, respectively. This suggests a lack of statistically significant changes in the presence of this grade over time. Additionally, the columns detailing the t-values and sample sizes (n) provide further context for the robustness of the analysis, with a sample size of 10 for each group. Overall, these findings contribute to our understanding of the stability of EZH2 grade 0 across the selected timeframes, offering insights into the potential implications for clinical outcomes and treatment strategies in relevant patient populations.
